reasoning: The question asks whether Nvidia will be permitted to sell its Blackwell chips (specified as B30A, B300, etc.) directly to China before May 2026. The U.S. government blocked the sale of the China-specific Blackwell model (the B30A) in November 2025. In January 2026, the Trump administration controversially lifted the AI export ban for the older Hopper-architecture H200 chips in exchange for a 25% tariff/surcharge, but this action faced significant bipartisan backlash. In remarks surrounding these policies, Trump explicitly stated that the most advanced chips (Blackwell) would be reserved for the U.S. and that nobody else would have access to them. As of late March 2026, financial reports confirm that only the H200 was permitted for sale, with the Blackwell architecture remaining banned. Given the intense political pressure, Trump's explicit statements restricting Blackwell, and the very short timeframe remaining until the May 2026 resolution date (only 18 days from the forecast date of April 12, 2026), it is highly unlikely the policy will be reversed in time. The market was correctly pricing this at 10% in early April, and with the passage of another 10 days without any news, the probability is now slightly lower.
